LAW AND MOTION 301, PLAINTIFF ZYNGA GAME NETWORK, INC.'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS DENIED. THE COURT THINKS THAT THE PLAINTIFF HAS NOT CARRIED ITS BURDEN OF SHOWING A SUFFICIENT PROBABILITY OF A SUCCESS ON THE MERITS TO WARRANT INTERIM RELIEF AT THIS TIME. THE COURT ORDERED MR. WEIL TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER. FEES ARE IMPOSED PURSUANT TO GC 68086FF AND CRC 892. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL A. KAREN CSR# 8189 (301/PJB)

LAW AND MOTION 301, AFTER THE ARGUMENT, DEFENDANTS CLZ CONCEPTS (DBA AFTERSHOCK INNOVATIONS LLC), JEFF CHEN, JAMES ZHANG'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY THE ACTION WAS TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION AND WILL STAND SUBMITTED AS OF MARCH 3, 2009. PLAINTIFF WAS ORDERED BY THE COURT TO SUBMIT SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEF ON THE ISSUE OF ARBITRABILITY ON THE NINTH CIRCUIT OPINION FROM JANUARY 29, 2009. EACH PARTY IS TO SUBMIT ANALYSIS OF WHETHER, IF THE COURT ORDERS ARBITRATION, IT ORDERS IT TO PROCEED THROUGH JAMS OR THROUGH AAA. EACH PARTY IS TO SUBMIT PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER WITH RESPECT TO THE PETITION TO ARBITRATE AND A STATEMENT CONCERNING JAMS VERSUS AAA. THE DAY FOR SUBMISSION IS ON OR BEFORE MARCH 3, 2009. THE MATTER IS CONTINUED TO MARCH 3, 2009. FEES ARE IMPOSED PURSUANT TO GC 68086FF AND CRC 892. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL A. KAREN CSR# 8189 (301/PJB)
